description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a brick exterior and a prominent gable roof covered in brown shingles. The house features a main rectangular structure with a lower, extended section on one side, creating a stepped facade. Windows are symmetrically arranged with colorful frames, and there are two visible entry doors at the front, suggesting a main entrance. The property includes a paved base, a small garage or carport on one side, and landscaped elements such as a tall, narrow tree and a bushy tree near the corners. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shade to the windows and entryways. The overall design is practical and residential, suitable for a family in a suburban neighborhood.
description: A two-story brick house with a gable roof, colorful window frames, and landscaped trees on a paved lot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with symmetrical windows, the main entrance, and two trees flanking the house.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the garage/carport, a tree near the corner, and the stepped extension of the lower level.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and the paved lot extending behind the house.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended lower section, colorful windows, and a bench near the wall.
attribute_entryway_design: Double front doors with a small overhang, no porch, and minimal decorative elements.
